channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸 Hegseth Faces Congressional Grilling Over Iran War
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testifies before Congress for the first time since the Iran war began, facing questions about the .5 trillion defense budget, costs of the conflict, and military preparedness.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Sentences Ex-President Yoon to 7 Years
item.description: Ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol receives a 7-year sentence for resisting arrest and bypassing Cabinet procedures before his martial law declaration, adding to his existing life sentence for rebellion.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Taiwan Tops Agenda for Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: China considers Taiwan its 'core of core interests' as Beijing prepares for a summit with Trump, while the US remains the island's most important international backer.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Feels Economic Strain From Iran War
item.description: British consumer confidence slumps to two-year low as the Iran war drives price increases, with PM Starmer refusing US requests to use UK bases for strikes on Iranian infrastructure.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Accuses Meta of Failing to Block Underage Users
item.description: The European Union charged Meta with violating digital rules by not preventing children under 13 from accessing Facebook and Instagram, threatening fines up to 6% of global revenue.

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE to Exit OPEC as Iran War Strains Gulf Oil Alliance
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced it will leave OPEC on May 1, dealing a major blow to the 65-year-old cartel as the Iran-US war disrupts Gulf energy flows and opens the door to a potential price war among producers.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇺🇸 King Charles Raises Iran Nuclear Concern at White House State Dinner
item.description: President Trump said Britain's King Charles does not want Iran to obtain nuclear weapons, bringing Middle East tensions into a state visit aimed at reaffirming US-UK ties amid deep divisions over the Iran war.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇵🇱 Belarus Frees Polish Journalist in Prisoner Swap With Poland
item.description: Belarus released Andrzej Poczobut, a prominent Polish-Belarusian journalist and activist, as part of a prisoner exchange at the border with Poland, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇦 Putin Accuses Ukraine of Intensifying Attacks on Russian Civilians
item.description: President Vladimir Putin said Ukraine has stepped up attacks on civilian infrastructure in Russia, citing a recent drone strike on an oil refinery in the southern city of Tuapse.

item.title: 🇲🇱⚔️ Mali Junta Leader Vows to Neutralize Attackers After Coordinated Offensive
item.description: Mali's military leader Assimi Goita made his first public appearance since insurgents launched coordinated attacks over the weekend, vowing to "neutralize" those responsible as he met with Russia's ambassador.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇷🇺 Russia as Economic Lifeline for Iran amid Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Iranian FM Araghchi visited St Petersburg to discuss sanctions and Hormuz with Putin, as Tehran explores railway and Caspian trade networks to bypass Gulf disruptions. Experts say Moscow could offer a lifeline but logistical issues and costs offer little long-term incentive.

item.title: 🌍🌡️ Europe Named Fastest-Warming Continent in UN Climate Report
item.description: World Meteorological Organization reports Europe experienced above-average temperatures in 2025, with record marine heatwaves and wildfires burning over 1 million hectares. Nearly 70% of European rivers recorded below-average annual flows.

item.title: 🇨🇳🚗 China Suspends New Autonomous Driving Permits After Baidu Incident
item.description: Beijing halted issuance of new robotaxi licenses after about 200 driverless vehicles suddenly stopped in Wuhan in March. The move clouds the outlook for Chinas autonomous vehicle sector.

item.title: 🇲🇽🇺🇸 Mexico Opens Investigation into Unauthorized CIA Agents in Country
item.description: Mexicos attorney general announced a full probe into two US CIA agents who died in a car crash while operating in the country without authorization. The incident raises questions about US intelligence activities in Mexico.

item.title: 🇦🇺🛢️ Australian State Launches First Gas Tender in a Decade
item.description: New South Wales opened its first natural gas exploration tender in ten years amid supply crunch from Middle East war. Entry fees were slashed from A$50,000 to A$1,000 to encourage participation. Queensland also pushed for fast-track approval of first new oil project in 50 years.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Cancels Military Equipment Display at Victory Day Parade Due to Ukraine War Losses
item.description: Russia will hold its annual Victory Day parade on Red Square without displaying military equipment, a stark acknowledgment of the drain on resources from the ongoing war in Ukraine that has depleted tanks and armored vehicles.

item.title: ⚡ World Bank Warns Energy Prices Could Surge 24% in 2026 Due to Iran War
item.description: The World Bank forecasts energy prices will reach their highest level since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by Middle East war disruptions even in the most optimistic scenario where conflict subsides by May.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Coast Guard Chief Visits Disputed South China Sea Islet Despite Vietnam Protests
item.description: Taiwan's Coast Guard minister visited a second Taiwan-controlled islet deep in the South China Sea, dismissing Vietnam's complaints and asserting Taiwan's presence in the contested waterway amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines Defense Chief Says Iran War Won't Distract US from Indo-Pacific
item.description: The Philippine defense secretary expressed confidence that US military capabilities in the Indo-Pacific remain strong despite Middle East commitments, while warning China could attempt to exploit any perceived opening.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Five Million Children in Darfur Face Extreme Hunger and Violence as Sudan War Enters Fourth Year
item.description: UNICEF issued an emergency warning over the humanitarian crisis in Sudan's Darfur region, where civil war has created devastating conditions for children facing extreme deprivation, hunger, and violence.

item.title: ⚖️ Purdue Pharma sentenced to $5.5B, OxyContin maker to dissolve
item.description: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma received a $5.5 billion sentence after pleading guilty to deceiving regulators and paying kickbacks to doctors. The sentencing paves the way for the companys dissolution and opioid settlements.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran IRGC tightens grip as war strains economy and blunts Supreme Leader role
item.description: Wartime pressure has concentrated power in Iran into a narrower, harder-line inner circle as the IRGC seizes control, blunting the Supreme Leaders role. Two months into the US-Israel war, the Revolutionary Guards dominance grows amid economic collapse.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Hezbollah drone kills Israeli contractor in southern Lebanon
item.description: An Israeli civilian contractor was killed and his son wounded by an FPV drone strike while operating an excavator in southern Lebanon, where IDF forces are demolishing Hezbollah infrastructure.

item.title: 🇪🇺🤝 US and NATO face frustrating marriage crisis, Belgian defense minister says
item.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken described strained transatlantic relations as a marriage crisis, urging NATO unity while acknowledging growing tensions in the alliance during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US ties UN dues payment to China curbs, report reveals
item.description: The United States, owing billions in unpaid fees to the UN, has reportedly attached conditions to its dues payment including measures targeting China, as the organization faces imminent financial collapse.
